description: xport lock-step manifest for socket-btm â€” the Build Tree Manager. Every row is a `version-pin` since btm builds upstream source trees as-is (no forks). The harness surfaces new upstream releases so maintainers can decide whether to bump. criticality reflects production impact: 10 = Firewall-critical (node runtime, networking stack); 8 = user-facing TUI / ML inference; 6 = build tooling; 4 = aux/testing.

rows.notes: node-smol-builder produces the slim Node.js runtime for Socket Firewall. Major bumps require wide testing (V8 API churn, perf regressions); patch/minor auto-track.

temporal_rs / temporal_capi crates back the Temporal global in Node 26+. Pinned to =0.1.0 by upstream Node's deps/crates/Cargo.toml â€” bumping requires a matching upstream Node bump, which is why this row is locked rather than track-latest. Vendored under the node submodule at deps/crates/vendor/temporal_rs/; configure.py asserts rustc/cargo >= 1.82 with LLVM >= 19. Smoke-tested by packages/build-infra/test/fixtures/smoke-test-modules.mjs.

deviations: C++ instead of Rust â€” port of `temporal_rs::Instant` to `node::socketsecurity::temporal::Instant`.
Uses V8's `v8::base::Time::Now()` for system clock instead of `std::time::SystemTime`. V8's facility is already linked into every Node binary; no syscall plumbing.

128-bit nanosecond storage via `Int128` shim (native `__int128` on GCC/Clang, `absl::int128` on MSVC) instead of Rust's `i128`. The MSVC fallback uses V8-vendored abseil at deps/v8/third_party/abseil-cpp/.

PARTIAL: only `Instant::Now()` and `Instant::IsValid()` are ported. `add`, `subtract`, `since`, `until`, `round`, `equals`, `toString`, `toJSON`, `compare`, `fromEpochMilliseconds`, `fromEpochNanoseconds`, `from`, `toZonedDateTimeISO` not yet ported.
rows.notes: First file in the temporal_rs â†’ C++ port lock-step (1:1 module-by-module mapping, idiomatic C++ where C++ idioms are clearer; same shape as ultrathink/acorn's Rustâ†’C++ port). Implementation work tracked in task #217; this row records the contract for drift audit on bumps to the upstream parity submodule.

deviations: C++ instead of Rust.

Plain C++ check (no Isolate) for `Duration::IsValid()`, mirroring spec's `IsValidDuration` directly. V8 also exposes `IsValidDuration(Isolate*, DurationRecord)` in `js-temporal-helpers.h`, but it requires an Isolate. Our path is callable from any context.
PARTIAL: only `IsValid()` is ported. `Duration::from`, `with`, `negated`, `abs`, `add`, `subtract`, `round`, `total`, `toString`, `toJSON`, `compare`, `valueOf`, `blank`, `sign` not yet ported.
rows.notes: Pairs with temporal-infra-instant. Same forked_at_sha. Tracks the same upstream submodule.

deviations: C++ instead of Rust.

Sakamoto's day-of-week algorithm and Fliegel-Van Flandern JDN formula (well-known math), not a literal translation of upstream's date helpers â€” matches the same outputs but is shorter and more readable in C++.

PARTIAL: `IsLeapYear`, `ISODaysInMonth`, `ISODayOfWeek`, `ISODayOfYear`, `ISOWeekOfYear`, `BalanceISODate`, `RegulateISODateConstrain`, `AddISODate`, `DifferenceISODate("day")` are ported. Spec helpers `BalanceISOYearMonth`, `IsoDateSurpasses`, `MoveRelativeDate`, `RoundRelativeDuration`, full `DifferenceISODate` with non-day largestUnit, and the comparison helpers are not yet ported.
rows.notes: Calendar-agnostic ISO 8601 math. Calendar-aware operations (non-Gregorian) defer to ICU via icu_calendar.cc (forthcoming, task #217).

deviations: C++ recursive-descent parser instead of upstream's `ixdtf`-backed parser (a separate Rust crate at https://github.com/boa-dev/ixdtf).

Hand-rolled cursor-based reader rather than any parser-combinator library. KISS over reuse here â€” the grammar is small enough (~80 productions, ~30 of which are common shapes) that a 1k-LOC C++ parser is cleaner than ~5k LOC of generated FFI bindings to the Rust ixdtf crate.

PARTIAL: covers the common shapes (DateSpec, TimeSpec, DateTime, UTC offsets, sub-second precision). Missing: RFC 9557 calendar/timezone annotations (`[u-ca=â€¦]`, `[Etc/UTC]`, `[!America/New_York]`), signed expanded years (`Â±YYYYYY`), fractional second in offset.
Returns `ParseStatus::kUnsupported` for valid-but-unimplemented productions so callers can surface to the user instead of failing silently.
rows.notes: ixdtf upstream itself is a separate boa-dev crate (https://github.com/boa-dev/ixdtf). If the C++ port grows to need full RFC 9557 coverage, consider a separate xport row tracking that upstream rather than vendoring ixdtf alongside temporal_rs.

deviations: ErrorKind / TemporalError / TemporalResult<T> mirror upstream 1:1. Static factories (Generic/Type/Range/Syntax/Assert) replace upstream's free constructors.
TemporalResult<T> uses a hand-rolled discriminated-union template (mirroring absl::StatusOr's surface) instead of std::variant or std::expected (C++23, not yet usable in V8's libnode build).

From<ParseError> / From<icu_calendar::Error> conversions are deferred â€” V8's existing js-temporal-objects.cc raises its own RangeError/TypeError, so we pass `kind` through and let the V8 layer translate.
rows.notes: Pairs with all other temporal-infra-* rows. Same forked_at_sha. Tracks the same upstream submodule.

deviations: Time-unit constants (kMsPerSecond/Hour/Day, kNsPerMicrosecond/Millisecond/Second/...) and the JDN â†” Gregorian helpers (EpochDaysFromGregorianDate / YmdFromEpochDays) are inlined here â€” upstream re-exports a few from `timezone_provider::utils`, which we don't port.
Implementation uses Fliegel-Van Flandern formula identically to upstream.
Three-trait shape (HostClock + HostTimeZone + HostHooks) collapsed into a single `HostHooks` abstract class with two virtuals â€” C++ has no trait composition.
EmptyHostSystem â†’ DefaultEmptyHostSystem: returns 0 epoch ns + a placeholder TimeZone error pending time_zone.cc.
Backed by V8's `v8::base::Time::Now()` (already linked into libnode) instead of std::SystemTime + iana_time_zone crate.
LocalHostSystem's IANA detection delegates to V8's `Intl::DefaultTimeZone()` once time_zone.cc lands â€” pending.
`Now<H: HostHooks>` template parameter replaced with a non-owning `HostHooks*` pointer (same flexibility, ABI-stable).
PARTIAL: methods that depend on PlainDate/PlainDateTime/ZonedDateTime/TimeZone return TemporalError until those classes land. InstantNow() is also pending Instant's ns-accepting constructor.
ParsedDate / ParsedDateTime mirror upstream 1:1. ParsedZonedDateTime is pending (depends on TimeZone class).
PARTIAL: YearMonthFromUtf8 / MonthDayFromUtf8 return TemporalError until parse.cc Phase 2 (RFC 9557 calendar annotations) lands.
PartialTime uses std::optional<T> instead of Rust's Option<T>.
Methods exposed as free functions (PlainTimeNew/PlainTimeTryNew/PlainTimeFromUtf8/PlainTimeWith) instead of member methods â€” keeps PlainTime POD-style (matches the rest of the temporal.h surface).
PARTIAL: Add/Subtract/Until/Since return TemporalError until duration normalization (TimeDuration) ports.
PARTIAL: ISO-only path. Calendar-aware methods (with, add, subtract, until, since, days_in_year, months_in_year) pending calendar.cc.
FromUtf8 falls back to '-01' day-pad until parse.cc Phase 2 supports the bare YYYY-MM grammar.
Reference year is 1972 (matches spec; leap year so Feb 29 is representable).
PARTIAL: ISO-only path. FromUtf8 falls back to '1972-' year-pad until parse.cc Phase 2 supports the bare MM-DD grammar.
Combines src/options.rs and src/options/increment.rs into one .cc â€” both define options-layer types and there's no benefit to splitting them in C++.

Enums use C++ scoped enums (enum class) with explicit numeric values matching upstream's discriminants (Auto=0, Nanosecond=1, â€¦, Year=10) so that LargerOf operations can rely on direct comparison, same as upstream's Ord derive.
FromStr/Display impls become free FromString/ToString functions â€” no Rust trait machinery in C++.
RoundingIncrement uses an internal uint32_t (1..=10^9) with the invariant enforced at construction (matches upstream's NonZeroU32 newtype with the same range).
RelativeTo (src/options/relative_to.rs) NOT yet ported â€” depends on TimeZone class. Lands with zoned_date_time.cc.

Upstream's `Display` impl for `Unit::Millisecond` returns the typo "millsecond" â€” preserved here for byte-level parity. The Display impl is internal-only; user-visible error messages use "millisecond".
rows.notes: Foundational â€” required by every file that consumes options (PlainDate, PlainTime, PlainDateTime, Duration, Instant, ZonedDateTime, etc.).

deviations: Header-only port â€” IncrementRounder<T> is a class template; no .cc.
Roundable Rust trait collapsed into free helpers in `rounding_internal::` namespace â€” one set for integral T, one for f64 (constexpr where possible).

Operates on int64_t instead of upstream's i128. Caller domain (nanosecond diffs â‰¤ 8.64e21) genuinely needs i128 only for the unusual extreme case; deferred until duration_normalized.cc forces the issue.
Negative dividend handling: emulates Rust's div_euclid (floor toward -inf) by adjusting the C++ truncating quotient when remainder is non-zero and signs disagree.
ISO-only path. PartialDate omits month_code / era / era_year (calendar-extension fields) â€” they are pending calendar.cc.
PlainDateAdd uses AddISODate (ISO calendar arithmetic). For non-ISO calendars upstream routes through Calendar::date_add; that path lands when calendar.cc lands.
PlainDateUntil/PlainDateSince emit days-only Durations (matching upstream's largestUnit=day default). Calendar-aware year/month/week breakdown is calendar.cc.
Subtract negates the input duration and routes to Add â€” same as upstream.
PartialDateTime decomposed into {date: PartialDate, time: PartialTime} â€” upstream uses a single DateTimeFields struct combining CalendarFields + PartialTime. Same shape, simpler C++ representation.
PlainDateTimeAdd / Subtract / Until / Since stub to TemporalError until duration_normalized.cc lands (depends on TimeDuration arithmetic).
FromUtf8 accepts strings with or without a UTC offset (the offset is permitted and ignored â€” the TZ-bearing variant is ZonedDateTime). Matches upstream.

ARCHITECTURAL: upstream wraps ICU4X's `AnyCalendar` (a Rust calendar library); this port delegates to system ICU (`icu::Calendar` from deps/icu-small) which V8 already links. Public surface (Calendar, CalendarKind, MonthCode, CalendarFields, YearMonthCalendarFields) matches upstream 1:1.

SCAFFOLD: only the ISO calendar path is implemented today. Non-ISO calendars (Buddhist, Chinese, Coptic, Dangi, Ethiopian, Gregorian, Hebrew, Indian, Hijri family, Japanese, Persian, ROC) surface 'Unsupported' until the ICU C-API dispatch lands.
TryFromUtf8 accepts the Temporal-allowed identifiers including aliases (gregoryâ†’Gregorian, islamic-civilâ†’HijriTabularFriday, ethioaaâ†’EthiopianAmeteAlem, etc.).
Identifier() returns the canonical lowercase IANA name.

ARCHITECTURAL: upstream consults the `timezone_provider` crate (bundled tzdata); this port delegates to V8's existing js-temporal-zoneinfo64.cc + system ICU (`icu::TimeZone`) â€” both already linked into libnode.

SCAFFOLD: offset-only zones work today (UtcOffset round-trip, Â±HH:MM[:SS[.fff]] parsing, ToString rendering). IANA-named zones (`America/New_York`, etc.) compile and round-trip the identifier but the actual offset/transition lookup is pending the V8 zoneinfo64 dispatch layer.
GetIsoDateTimeFor on offset zones is currently stubbed â€” the Int128 arithmetic for adding the offset to an Instant lands once the broader Int128 helpers settle (see temporal_int128.h).

SCAFFOLD: structural surface (ZonedDateTime { instant, time_zone, calendar }) plus thin field accessors and to_plain_*  conversions. Most operations route through TimeZone::GetIsoDateTimeFor which is itself scaffolded â€” full IANA path lands when V8 zoneinfo64 dispatch is wired up.
FromUtf8 currently handles only UTC-Z input (no [TimeZone]/[!TimeZone] annotation), defaulting to UTC zone + ISO calendar. Full RFC 9557 [TimeZone] annotation support is parse.cc Phase 2.
DST + offset disambiguation (the bulk of upstream's zoned_date_time.rs â€” ~2.5k LOC) is pending. Operations that need it (since/until/round/with) stub to TemporalError.
Tagged-union RelativeTo (PlainDate | ZonedDateTime). Mirrors upstream's `enum RelativeTo` 1:1.
TryFromUtf8 tries ZonedDateTime first then falls back to PlainDate. Today the ZonedDateTime path matches only UTC-Z input (parse.cc Phase 2 work); IANA-annotated paths surface 'Unsupported' for now.

SCAFFOLD: defines DateDuration / TimeDuration / InternalDurationRecord types + sign/abs/negate/from-components helpers. The full arithmetic pipeline (round / balance / total / normalize, ~1.5k LOC) is the hardest part of the port and lands in a separate phase â€” it depends on Int128 multiplication/division (added to temporal_int128.h here) and the rounding.h template instantiations being plumbed through.
DateDuration uses int64_t fields matching upstream exactly. TimeDuration wraps Int128 (via temporal_int128.h's wrapper).
Folds upstream's two files (duration/date.rs + duration/normalized.rs) into a single .cc â€” both define duration-record types and there's no benefit to splitting them in C++.
MAX_TIME_DURATION constant is computed at runtime via Int128 arithmetic (no constexpr i128 literals in standard C++).
FiniteF64 + DoubleDouble live entirely inline / templated in primitive.h. The .cc is a stub translation unit anchoring the gyp source list and reserving a home for any future out-of-line definitions.

Numeric-conversion machinery (Rust's num_traits FromPrimitive / AsPrimitive / PrimInt traits) collapsed into C++ template member functions on FiniteF64 (AsIntegerIfIntegral<T>, AsIntegerWithTruncation<T>, AsPositiveIntegerWithTruncation<T>) â€” same surface, no trait machinery.

rows.notes:
Phase 9 closed the file-by-file 1:1 port AND activated the integration layer. Status is now:

- TimeDuration arithmetic pipeline: full (round / balance / total / from_components / from_duration / from_nanosecond_difference / add_days / divide / negate / checked_add / checked_sub / checked_add_ns / round_to_fractional_days / seconds / subseconds / sign).
- IsoTime helpers: full (BalanceIsoTime / DiffIsoTime / AddIsoTime / RoundIsoTime).
- PlainTime: full (try_new / new_with_overflow / from_partial / from_utf8 / with / add / subtract / until / since / round).
- PlainDateTime: full (try_new / new_with_overflow / from_partial / from_utf8 / with / accessors / to_plain_date / to_plain_time / add / subtract / until / since).
- PlainDate: full (try_new_iso / new_with_overflow / from_partial / from_utf8 / with / accessors / add / subtract / until / since).
- PlainYearMonth / PlainMonthDay: full (try_new_iso / from_utf8 / accessors).
- IXDTF parser: full RFC 9557 coverage (signed expanded years, calendar annotations, time-zone annotations, sub-second offset fractions, critical `!` markers, bare YearMonth + MonthDay grammars).
- ZonedDateTime: full FromUtf8 with [TimeZone]/[u-ca=â€¦] annotation support; conversion to Instant/PlainDate/PlainTime/PlainDateTime.
- Now / Host / Sys: full â€” system clock via v8::base::Time::Now; UTC singleton + LocalHostSystem (TZ env var path).
- TimeZone: full surface. Offset zones do GetIsoDateTimeFor inline via Int128. IANA zones delegate to the registered TimeZoneBackend.
- Calendar: full surface. ISO arithmetic inline. Non-ISO calendars delegate to the registered CalendarBackend.

What the hosting embedder must provide:
1. A `TimeZoneBackend` impl (e.g. zoneinfo64-backed via deps/v8/src/objects/js-temporal-zoneinfo64.cc). Register via `SetTimeZoneBackend()`.
2. A `CalendarBackend` impl (e.g. ICU-backed via deps/icu-small). Register via `SetCalendarBackend()`.
3. (Optional) A custom `HostHooks` impl to override the system clock / system zone for tests.

These backends are the V8/Isolate-bound integration layer; temporal-infra is intentionally Isolate-free so the algorithmic core can be unit-tested without spinning V8.

Upstream's `src/builtins/compiled/*` files are intentionally NOT ported â€” they are thin TZ_PROVIDER convenience wrappers. V8 plumbs the provider through Isolate; the convenience layer is unnecessary in the C++ embed.

Upstream's `calendar/fields.rs` + `calendar/types.rs` internal helpers (FieldKeysToIgnore, ResolutionType, check_year_in_safe_arithmetical_range, month_to_month_code) live in the future ICU-backed CalendarBackend impl, not in temporal-infra itself.

Total upstream Rust LOC ported file-by-file: ~10k of ~14k non-test.
Linux io_uring bindings compiled into node-smol. Security-sensitive kernel interface.
Low-level networking primitive underlying uWebSockets in node-smol.
HTTP/WebSocket server in node-smol. API compatibility across majors is brittle.
libcurl statically linked. Frequent CVEs â€” prefer latest stable.
TLS library under curl. Crypto/security sensitive.
ML inference runtime for embedded models. Major bumps can break model compatibility.
Native opentui builder. Matches the pin in socket-tui's xport for consistency across the OpenTUI ecosystem.

Charmbracelet Ultraviolet â€” terminal I/O + decoder foundation under Bubble Tea v2 / Lip Gloss v2. Pre-1.0, fast-moving, no release tags. ultraviolet-builder wraps it via napi-go. Consumed by socket-tui for kitty/fixterms/SGR keyboard + mouse decoding.
React-for-terminal library builder.
Rust-based terminal UI framework builder.
Yoga layout WASM builder. Matches stuie's pin for cross-repo yoga consistency.
Binary inspection library used by binject/binflate.
DEFLATE compression in binject. Track-latest for speed/size improvements.
JSON parser in binject. Small surface, stable API.
Zstandard compression in bin-infra.
Source tree for libpq-builder. Only libpq client lib is extracted.
Web Platform Tests for node-smol's fast-webstreams vendor. Epoch-based (rolling).

Reference JS semver. We port the parser + comparators + range expander to C++ in src/socketsecurity/versions/ and expose the result as node:smol-versions. When semver bumps, re-run the cross-checked test corpus to confirm the C++ port still matches. See docs/ports/semver.md for the port mapping.

deviations: Single-pass byte scanner (upstream is regex-driven).
C++ struct stores prerelease/build as offsets into the source buffer; upstream stores arrays of strings.
Loose-mode whitespace is more conservative â€” leading 'v'/'=' and surrounding whitespace only; upstream's loose mode is broader.
Identifier comparison uses memcmp with explicit length-then-bytes tiebreak.
Numeric/alphanumeric distinction uses a single-pass digit predicate; upstream uses a regex test.
Caret/tilde/hyphen expansions emit comparators directly into a Range struct rather than going through string assembly + re-parse.
Comparator op is a uint8_t enum (0..6) instead of an operator string.
X-range / wildcard handling collapses 'x', 'X', and '*' into a single kXAny sentinel.
